results.rendered_html:
ul-√las (ud-√las) P. Ā. -lasati, -te, to shine forth, beam, radiate, be brilliant,  BhP. ;  Pañcar. ;  Śiś.  &c.;
to come forth, become visible or perceptible, appear,  BhP. ;  Kathās.  &c.;
to resound,  Kathās. ;  Rājat. ;
to sport, play, dance, be wanton or joyful,  Amar. ;  Chandom. ;
to jump, shake, tremble, be agitated,  BhP. ;  Pañcar.  &c.:
Caus. -lāsayati, to cause to shine or radiate, make brilliant,  Pañcar. ;  Prab. ;
to cause to come forth or appear, cause to resound,  Sāh. ;
to divert, delight,  Śatr. ;  Hit. ;
to cause to dance or jump, agitate, cause to move,  Kathās. ;  Rājat. ;  Hit.  &c.

results.rendered_html:
ullas 1 P.
1 To jump up, sport, play, wave, flutter; ullasitāsitapatākāsahasraṃ  K. 96 ;  Śi. 5. 47 , 53. 
2 To flash, shine, glitter, sparkle; ullasatkāṃcanakuṃḍalāgraṃ  Śi. 3. 5 , 33 , 5. 55 ; 16. 61 ; 20. 56 ; (fig.) to brighten or cheer up;  K. 189. 
3 To rise, appear forth;  Śi. 4. 58 , 6. 51 , 16. 51 ;  Māl. 9. 38. 
4 To be reflected;  Śi. 11. 54. 
5 To blow. open, be expanded.
— Caus.
1 To cause to jump up or play.
2 To brighten, illuminate, grace;  Ve. 1. 12 ;  U. 4. 
3 To
(pb) divert, delight.
4 To shake, move  K. 22 ;  Ki. 16. 37.
